channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: 27-Year-Old Woman Dies After Acid Attack in Mbeere
item.description: In Kiritiri, Mbeere South, a tragic incident occurred on Saturday, May 16, when 27-year-old Jackline Wanjiru lost her life following a violent acid attack. She was on her way to Kiritiri Market to sel...

item.title: Diesel prices projected to increase by nearly P3 in late May
item.description: Diesel fuel prices in the Philippines are expected to increase by almost P3 per liter in the third week of May 2026, continuing the upward trend seen in recent weeks. This adjustment is influenced by ...

item.title: SNL UK ends first series with modest ratings but secures second series
item.description: The first series of Saturday Night Live UK concluded with its lowest audience figures yet, facing strong competition from the Eurovision Song Contest. The finale, hosted by Sex Education actor Ncuti G...

item.title: Newlands Hosts Flood Relief Donations Drive Supported by WPCA and Gift of the Givers
item.description: The Western Province Cricket Association (WPCA) has partnered with humanitarian organisation Gift of the Givers to provide relief for communities in the Western Cape affected by severe floods. Newland...

item.title: Two Teenagers Arrested After Multiple Random Shootings in Austin Injure Four
item.description: Over the weekend in Austin, Texas, a series of random shootings left four people injured and caused widespread fear across parts of the city. Two teenagers, ages 15 and 17, were taken into custody aft...

item.title: Eddie Nketia sets new all-conditions 100m sprint record
item.description: Eddie Nketia, a sprinter from Canberra, has established himself as Australia's fastest man in all conditions after clocking 9.74 seconds in a 100-metre sprint in the United States. Although the tailwi...

item.title: UK Businesses Delay Investment and Hiring Amid Rising Costs from Middle East Conflict
item.description: UK businesses are increasingly cautious in their investment and hiring decisions due to the escalating Iran conflict, according to recent surveys. More than half of medium-sized firms cited rising ene...

item.title: Mamata Banerjee Criticises Eviction Drives, Stresses Compassionate Governance in Bengal
item.description: Former Chief Minister and Trinamool Congress leader Mamata Banerjee has strongly criticised the recent eviction drives happening in various parts of West Bengal. In a social media post on May 16, 2026...

item.title: Trump Issues Warning to Iran Following Drone Attack on UAE Nuclear Plant
item.description: Former US President Donald Trump has issued a stern warning to Iran, asserting that 'the clock is ticking' and urging Tehran to reach a peace agreement promptly. Speaking to French media and posting o...

item.title: Celebrating Contemporary Street Photography: 250 Inspiring Examples Worldwide
item.description: Street photography remains a vibrant and evolving form of visual art, capturing humanity in all its complexity. LensCulture highlights 250 contemporary street photographs selected by Jim Casper, edito...

item.title: An Overview of Lee Radziwill’s Fashion Evolution in the 1960s and 1970s
item.description: Lee Radziwill, born on March 3, 1933, and sister of Jacqueline Kennedy, was renowned for her distinctive and daring fashion sense, often considered more avant-garde than her sister's iconic style. In ...

item.title: Trump intends to install a permanent helipad at the White House to prevent lawn damage
item.description: President Donald Trump has announced plans to construct a permanent helipad at the White House, citing a recurring problem with the new Marine One helicopters. For years, U.S. presidents have boarded ...

item.title: Kiwi tourist fined for entering Rome's Trevi Fountain
item.description: A New Zealand tourist has been fined after jumping into Rome's iconic Trevi Fountain, a popular attraction known for its rich history and cultural significance. Authorities have strict rules to protec...

item.title: Learn Today’s Filipino Word: 'Taliambong' Explained
item.description: ABS-CBN Lifestyle shares the featured Word of the Day for May 18, 2026, which is 'taliambong'. This initiative aims to enrich the Filipino vocabulary and help readers understand unique words from loca...

item.title: Iraqi National Charged in U.S. with Terrorism Connected to Toronto Consulate Shooting
item.description: Mohammad Baqer Saad Dawood Al-Saadi, a 32-year-old Iraqi national, is now in U.S. custody facing six terrorism-related charges, including conspiring to support terrorist acts and attempting to murder ...

item.title: Cosmo Industries Celebrates Disability Service Professionals Week in Saskatoon
item.description: Cosmo Industries in Saskatoon has been supporting individuals with intellectual disabilities since 1971, providing them with meaningful work opportunities and community programming. More than 400 part...

item.title: Upcoming CGT Changes in 2027: What Property Investors Need to Know
item.description: Australian property investors are facing significant tax changes coming into effect from 1 July 2027. The government will modify the capital gains tax (CGT) discount, meaning that gains on properties ...

item.title: UAE condemns drone strike near Barakah Nuclear Power Plant, no casualties reported
item.description: On Sunday, a drone strike in Abu Dhabi caused a fire near the Barakah Nuclear Power Plant, though fortunately no one was injured and radiation levels were unaffected. The UAE authorities confirmed tha...

item.title: BJP claims recovery of voter ID and Aadhaar cards from TMC offices in West Bengal
item.description: In West Bengal, several Trinamool Congress (TMC) offices were found to contain hundreds of voter IDs, Aadhaar cards, and MNREG Scheme job cards. These documents, recovered by local residents and Bhara...

item.title: Four Navy jet crew members safely eject after crash at Idaho air show
item.description: During an air show in Idaho, two Navy jets collided mid-flight, resulting in both aircraft crashing. Fortunately, all four crew members were able to eject safely and were reported to be unharmed. The ...
